title: platform-user — HTTP middleware
description: Two middleware unique to the platform_admin plane. `platform.audit` stamps EVERY platform-plane write with an audit row + correlation id — heavier than the tenant plane's default, per SOC 2 CC7.2. `platform.mfa_required` enforces MFA at request time regardless of the underlying user's MFA state — the platform_admin guard has no opt-out.

middleware.description: Wraps the response cycle. On every request that reaches this middleware (mounted on all /api/v1/platform/* routes), a correlation_id UUID is generated + attached to the request context. Every audit + activity row written during the request lifecycle inherits that correlation_id (foundation::HasActivityLog + audit::HasAudit read from the request context on write). On response emission, a single `platform.request.audit` activity_log entry is written with the method, path, status code, actor_id (PlatformUser.id), correlation_id, duration_ms, and IP (truncated to /24 per GDPR). Compliance evidence for SOC 2 CC7.2 — shows the full stream of platform-admin actions with per-request grouping. Skips on GETs that touch no PII AND resolve in < 100ms (audit-row volume optimisation — read-only telemetry is captured via metrics instead).

Refuses the request when the authenticated PlatformUser's referenced Identity has NULL mfa_secret_encrypted. Fires BEFORE the route matches so a request with a stale token (issued when the Identity had MFA, then MFA disabled server-side) fails at the middleware layer before reaching the controller. Distinct from the identity/auth login flow which requires MFA at token-issuance time — this middleware handles the runtime enforcement (config('platform-user.mfa.require_on_every_request')). Refusal shape: HTTP 401 with body `{ error: { code: 'PLATFORM_MFA_REQUIRED', message: 'Platform admin sessions require enrolled MFA; please re-enrol via the identity-service.' } }`.

consumed.description: Standard Sanctum guard resolver on the platform_admin guard. Runs BEFORE platform.mfa_required + platform.audit — they need the authenticated PlatformUser on the request context.

boundary_note: Both middlewares refuse to run on `sanctum`-guarded routes. The `platform.audit` middleware writes to the same audit + activity tables the tenant plane uses, but every row it writes carries `user_type = App\PlatformUser\Models\PlatformUser` so a tenant-plane audit-report query filters platform-plane rows out unless the caller explicitly asks for them (platform-admin surfaces do).
